pages.text: Biomancy's machines are not built from iron and redstone. They are grown — and like anything grown, each needs a living core and a steady diet.
pages.title: Every Machine Has a Heart

pages.text: $(bold)The core.$() Every machine carries one $(l:2_cradle/living_flesh)Living Flesh$() at its centre. That is what makes it a machine and not a pile of meat. Plan your Living Flesh supply around how many machines you intend to grow.$(br2)$(bold)The diet.$() Once running, the machines spend $(l:3_machines/nutrients)Nutrients$() as fuel. A machine with an empty Nutrient reserve simply stops — it will tell you, $(italic)"Not enough Nutrients."$()
pages.title: Core & Diet

pages.text: $(bold)The build order.$() The $(l:3_machines/bio_forge)Bio-Forge$() comes first — it is built at a vanilla bench and unlocks the rest. The $(l:3_machines/decomposer)Decomposer$() can also be bench-crafted, but the $(l:3_machines/digester)Digester$() and $(l:3_machines/bio_lab)Bio-Lab$() exist $(italic)only$() as Bio-Forge recipes — so the Forge is genuinely the gate to the rest of the craft.
pages.title: Where to Start
